Cardiovascular.name: Hypertension
Cardiovascular.description: High blood pressure that strains blood vessels.

Cardiovascular.name: Hyperlipidemia
Cardiovascular.description: High cholesterol or triglycerides in the blood.

Cardiovascular.name: Coronary Artery Disease (CAD)
Cardiovascular.description: Narrowing of the heart's primary arteries.

Cardiovascular.name: Heart Failure
Cardiovascular.description: Inability of the heart to pump blood effectively.

Cardiovascular.name: Arrhythmia
Cardiovascular.description: Irregular heartbeat rhythms, including atrial fibrillation.

Cardiovascular.name: Angina
Cardiovascular.description: Chest pain caused by reduced blood flow to the heart.

Cardiovascular.name: Peripheral Artery Disease (PAD)
Cardiovascular.description: Narrowed blood vessels reducing limb circulation.

Cardiovascular.name: Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT)
Cardiovascular.description: Blood clots forming in deep veins, typically legs.

Cardiovascular.name: Myocardial Infarction
Cardiovascular.description: Commonly known as a heart attack.

Cardiovascular.name: Stroke
Cardiovascular.description: Interrupted blood flow to the brain, causing tissue damage.

Respiratory.name: Influenza (Flu)
Respiratory.description: Highly contagious viral infection of the respiratory system.

Respiratory.name: Asthma
Respiratory.description: Chronic inflammation making the airways narrow and swell.

Respiratory.name: Acute Bronchitis
Respiratory.description: Temporary inflammation of the main airways in the lungs.

Respiratory.name: Pneumonia
Respiratory.description: Infection that inflames air sacs in one or both lungs.

Respiratory.name: Sinusitis
Respiratory.description: Swelling and inflammation of the nasal sinuses.

Respiratory.name: COVID-19
Respiratory.description: Respiratory illness caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus.

Metabolic & Endocrine.name: Type 2 Diabetes
Metabolic & Endocrine.description: Chronic condition affecting how the body processes blood sugar.

Metabolic & Endocrine.name: Type 1 Diabetes
Metabolic & Endocrine.description: Autoimmune condition where the pancreas produces little to no insulin.

Metabolic & Endocrine.name: Hypothyroidism
Metabolic & Endocrine.description: Underactive thyroid gland producing insufficient hormones.

Metabolic & Endocrine.name: Hyperthyroidism
Metabolic & Endocrine.description: Overactive thyroid gland producing too much hormone.

Metabolic & Endocrine.name: Obesity
Metabolic & Endocrine.description: Excessive body fat that increases other health risks.

Metabolic & Endocrine.name: Prediabetes
Metabolic & Endocrine.description: Elevated blood sugar levels not yet high enough for a diabetes diagnosis.

Metabolic & Endocrine.name: Metabolic Syndrome
Metabolic & Endocrine.description: Cluster of increasing heart disease and stroke risk.

Metabolic & Endocrine.name: Vitamin D Deficiency
Metabolic & Endocrine.description: Insufficient vitamin levels impacting bone and immune health.

Metabolic & Endocrine.name: Gout
Metabolic & Endocrine.description: Form of inflammatory arthritis tied to high uric acid.

Metabolic & Endocrine.name: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)
Metabolic & Endocrine.description: Hormonal disorder causing enlarged ovaries with small cysts.

Mental and Behavioral Health.name: Major Depressive Disorder (MDD)
Mental and Behavioral Health.description: Persistent feelings of sadness and loss of interest.

Mental and Behavioral Health.name: Panic Disorder
Mental and Behavioral Health.description: Sudden, recurring episodes of intense fear (panic attacks).

Mental and Behavioral Health.name: Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD)
Mental and Behavioral Health.description: Mental health condition triggered by terrifying events.

Mental and Behavioral Health.name: Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD)
Mental and Behavioral Health.description: Persistent patterns of inattention or hyperactivity.

Mental and Behavioral Health.name: Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder (OCD)
Mental and Behavioral Health.description: Unwanted, recurring thoughts leading to repetitive behaviors.

Mental and Behavioral Health.name: Bipolar Disorder
Mental and Behavioral Health.description: Brain disorder causing severe shifts in mood, energy, and activity.

Mental and Behavioral Health.name: Insomnia
Mental and Behavioral Health.description: Chronic difficulty falling or staying asleep.

Mental and Behavioral Health.name: Substance Use Disorder
Mental and Behavioral Health.description: Dependence on drugs, alcohol, or medications.

Mental and Behavioral Health.name: Social Anxiety Disorder
Mental and Behavioral Health.description: Intense fear of being watched and judged by others.

Musculoskeletal.name: Osteoarthritis
Musculoskeletal.description: Degenerative joint disease causing cartilage breakdown.

Musculoskeletal.name: Osteoporosis
Musculoskeletal.description: Weakened, brittle bones prone to fracture.

Musculoskeletal.name: Non-Specific Back Pain
Musculoskeletal.description: Pain in the lumbar or thoracic spine region.

Musculoskeletal.name: Fibromyalgia
Musculoskeletal.description: Widespread musculoskeletal pain accompanied by fatigue and sleep issues.

Musculoskeletal.name: Sciatica
Musculoskeletal.description: Pain radiating along the sciatic nerve down the legs.

Musculoskeletal.name: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome
Musculoskeletal.description: Compression of the median nerve in the wrist.

Musculoskeletal.name: Plantar Fasciitis
Musculoskeletal.description: Inflammation of tissue along the bottom of the foot.

Musculoskeletal.name: Tendinitis
Musculoskeletal.description: Inflammation or irritation of a tendon from overuse.

Musculoskeletal.name: Bursitis
Musculoskeletal.description: Painful condition affecting the fluid-filled sacs protecting joints.

Gastrointestinal.name: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD)
Gastrointestinal.description: Chronic acid reflux irritating the food pipe.

Gastrointestinal.name: Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS)
Gastrointestinal.description: Common disorder affecting the large intestine.

Gastrointestinal.name: Gastritis
Gastrointestinal.description: Inflammation of the protective stomach lining.

Gastrointestinal.name: Gastroenteritis
Gastrointestinal.description: Intestinal infection causing diarrhea and cramps (stomach flu).

Gastrointestinal.name: Hemorrhoids
Gastrointestinal.description: Swollen veins in the lowest part of the rectum and anus.

Gastrointestinal.name: Constipation
Gastrointestinal.description: Infrequent bowel movements or difficult passage of stool.

Gastrointestinal.name: Gallstones
Gastrointestinal.description: Hardened deposits of digestive fluid in the gallbladder.

Gastrointestinal.name: Celiac Disease
Gastrointestinal.description: Immune reaction to eating gluten that damages the small intestine.

Gastrointestinal.name: Crohn's Disease
Gastrointestinal.description: Type of inflammatory bowel disease causing tract inflammation.

Gastrointestinal.name: Ulcerative Colitis
Gastrointestinal.description: Inflammatory bowel disease causing long-lasting ulcers.

Dermatological.name: Acne Vulgaris
Dermatological.description: Skin condition occurring when hair follicles plug with oil.

Dermatological.name: Eczema (Atopic Dermatitis)
Dermatological.description: Condition making skin red and itchy.

Dermatological.name: Psoriasis
Dermatological.description: Skin disease causing red, itchy, scaly patches.

Dermatological.name: Contact Dermatitis
Dermatological.description: Red, itchy rash caused by direct contact with a substance.

Dermatological.name: Rosacea
Dermatological.description: Common skin condition causing blushing or flushing in the face.

Dermatological.name: Alopecia Areata
Dermatological.description: Autoimmune condition causing patchy hair loss.

Dermatological.name: Onychomycosis
Dermatological.description: Fungal infection of the fingernails or toenails.

Dermatological.name: Hives (Urticaria)
Dermatological.description: Itchy, raised welts on the skin surface.

Dermatological.name: Cellulitis
Dermatological.description: Common, potentially serious bacterial skin infection.

Dermatological.name: Warts
Dermatological.description: Small, grainy skin growths caused by human papillomavirus (HPV).

Neurological.name: Migraine
Neurological.description: Headache type causing severe throbbing pain or pulsing sensations.

Neurological.name: Tension Headaches
Neurological.description: Mild to moderate pain often described as a tight band around the head.

Neurological.name: Alzheimer's Disease
Neurological.description: Progressive neurologic disorder causing brain atrophy and memory loss.

Neurological.name: Vascular Dementia
Neurological.description: Memory loss caused by reduced blood flow to the brain.

Neurological.name: Epilepsy
Neurological.description: Central nervous system disorder causing recurrent seizures.

Neurological.name: Parkinson's Disease
Neurological.description: Progressive nervous system disorder affecting movement.

Neurological.name: Multiple Sclerosis (MS)
Neurological.description: Disease where the immune system eats away at nerve protection.

Neurological.name: Peripheral Neuropathy
Neurological.description: Nerve damage causing weakness, numbness, and pain in hands or feet.

Neurological.name: Essential Tremor
Neurological.description: Neurological disorder causing involuntary, rhythmic shaking.

Neurological.name: Vertigo (Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo)
Neurological.description: Sensation of spinning caused by inner ear issues.

Urological and Reproductive.name: Urinary Tract Infection (UTI)
Urological and Reproductive.description: Infection in any part of the urinary system.

Urological and Reproductive.name: Urinary Incontinence
Urological and Reproductive.description: Loss of bladder control causing accidental leakage.

Urological and Reproductive.name: Kidney Stones
Urological and Reproductive.description: Hard deposits of minerals and acid salts forming inside kidneys.

Urological and Reproductive.name: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKG)
Urological and Reproductive.description: Gradual loss of kidney function over time.

Urological and Reproductive.name: Erectile Dysfunction (ED)
Urological and Reproductive.description: Inability to get or keep an erection firm enough for intercourse.

Urological and Reproductive.name: Endometriosis
Urological and Reproductive.description: Tissue similar to the uterine lining growing outside the uterus.

Urological and Reproductive.name: Uterine Fibroids
Urological and Reproductive.description: Non-cancerous growths of the uterus during childbearing years.

Urological and Reproductive.name: Bacterial Vaginosis
Urological and Reproductive.description: Vaginal inflammation caused by overgrowth of natural bacteria.

Urological and Reproductive.name: Vaginal Candidiasis
Urological and Reproductive.description: Fungal infection commonly called a vaginal yeast infection.

Infectious and Immunological.name: Conjunctivitis
Infectious and Immunological.description: Inflammation of the transparent membrane lining the eyelid (pink eye).

Infectious and Immunological.name: Otitis Media
Infectious and Immunological.description: Middle ear infection common in pediatric patients.

Infectious and Immunological.name: Streptococcal Pharyngitis
Infectious and Immunological.description: Bacterial throat infection commonly known as strep throat.

Infectious and Immunological.name: Herpes Simplex (Oral or Genital)
Infectious and Immunological.description: Viral infection causing sores on lips or genitals.

Infectious and Immunological.name: Human Papillomavirus (HPV)
Infectious and Immunological.description: Viral infection passed through skin-to-skin contact.

Infectious and Immunological.name: Chlamydia
Infectious and Immunological.description: Common sexually transmitted infection that may not show symptoms.

Infectious and Immunological.name: Shingles (Herpes Zoster)
Infectious and Immunological.description: Viral infection causing a painful, localized rash.

Infectious and Immunological.name: Lyme Disease
Infectious and Immunological.description: Tick-borne bacterial illness causing fever and rashes.

Infectious and Immunological.name: Mononucleosis
Infectious and Immunological.description: Infectious disease often transmitted through saliva (mono).

Infectious and Immunological.name: HIV/AIDS
Infectious and Immunological.description: Chronic, life-threatening condition caused by the human immunodeficiency virus.
